g2_pseudoreplication.detail: The stratified split treats each row as an independent statistical unit. If rows share grouping structure (repeated measures, clustered sampling, time-series autocorrelation) the effective sample size is smaller than n_test and reported metrics will overstate generalisation. The engine cannot detect grouping from a flat source; the human reviewer must confirm independence.
g2_pseudoreplication.reference: Forstmeier, Wagenmakers & Parker (2017) Proc. R. Soc. B 284:20152463

g3_minimum_detectable_effect.detail: With n_test independent observations the minimum Cohen's h detectable at power=0.8 and alpha=0.05 (two-tailed) is mde_cohen_h. Effect sizes smaller than this threshold cannot be reliably distinguished from chance at the given sample size. Disclosure only — never a gate.
g3_minimum_detectable_effect.reference: Cohen, J. (1988) Statistical Power Analysis for the Behavioral Sciences (2nd ed.).

findings.leakage_check: per-feature association with the target (Cramér's V for categoricals, absolute point-biserial for numerics); associations at or above the fixed threshold are flagged possible_target_leakage - a warning for human judgment, never a gate. Motivated by a production run where a post-hoc label column produced a perfect score.
findings.note: Deterministic baseline: fixed integer seeds on splitter and estimator per scikit-learn's controlling-randomness guidance; metrics rounded to 6 decimals as a declared contract. This is a reference point for human modeling work, not a delivered model.
findings.target_selection: first binary_target in plan column order (plan approved at Human Gate 1); candidates listed under target_candidates
